topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative amendments and reforms addressing federal laws across multiple sectors including election, taxation, health, and law enforcement.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to extend through calendar 2013 the authority of the Federal Election Commission (FEC) to impose civil monetary penalties.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend to all domestic corporations, including S corporations, provisions allowing deferral of tax on gain from the sale of employer securities to an S corporation-sponsored employer stock ownership plan (ESOP).
Document 3: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to add New York State as a member of the New England Fishery Management Council.
Document 4: Amends the federal criminal code to authorize a probation officer to arrest a person without a warrant if there is probable cause to believe the person forcibly assaulted or obstructed a probation officer.
Document 5: Repeals the federal estate, gift, and generation-skipping transfer taxes.
Document 6: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award a grant to each eligible school of nursing.
Document 7: Amends the federal criminal code to impose a fine and/or prison term for knowingly operating a vessel without nationality for drug trafficking.

topic.name: Medicare Health Care Reforms
topic.description: Legislative amendments aimed at improving Medicare coverage and services for various health care needs.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide for coverage of comprehensive cancer care planning services.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to eligible entities to improve the quality of cancer care plans.
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to study whether there should be an increase in the prospective payment rate for inpatient rehabilitation services.
Directs the Secretary also to study the access by individuals to rehabilitation care in rural communities.
Document 3: Amends part B (Supplementary Medical Insurance Benefits for Aged and Disabled) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to repeal the Medicare competitive acquisition program for durable medical equipment.
Rescinds specified unobligated balances of all discretionary appropriations.
Document 4: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to cover low vision devices under Medicare.
Document 5: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to permit a prescription drug plan sponsor to offer coverage of covered part D drugs for medically accepted indications.

topic.name: Educational Support and Funding
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving educational systems through funding, support for teachers, and community involvement.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to consortia composed of local educational agencies and community-based organizations.
Requires schools to provide access to educational, developmental, family, health, and other comprehensive services.
Document 2: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require the Department of Education to provide for the use of tax information to determine a student's financial aid eligibility.
Document 3: Provides up to $17,500 of loan forgiveness to highly qualified teachers employed in a teacher shortage area.
Document 4: Allows the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make demonstration grants to reduce the student-to-school nurse ratio.
Document 5: Expands loan forgiveness available to public school teachers under the Higher Education Act of 1965.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive matching grants for the development of afterschool interdisciplinary education programs.
Document 7: Authorizes competitive grants to improve high school graduation rates and college preparedness.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension and Amendments
topic.description: Legislative measures related to the temporary suspension or amendment of duties on specific goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: extends the temporary suspension of duty on 2-methyl-4-methoxy-6-methylamino-1,3,5-triazine.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: renews the temporary suspension of duty on macroporous ion-exchange resin comprising a copolymer of styrene crosslinked with divinylbenzene, thiol functionalized.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: suspends temporarily the duty on certain synthetic staple fibers that are not carded, combed, or otherwise processed for spinning.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 4: extends the suspension of duty on macroporous ion-exchange resin comprising a copolymer of styrene crosslinked with divinylbenzene, thiol functionalized.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 5: suspends temporarily the duty on 4-Vinylbenzenesulfonic acid, lithium salt.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 6: suspends temporarily the duty on certain work boots for men.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 7: reduces temporarily the duty on certain hybrid electric vehicle inverters.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Programs
topic.description: Legislation and initiatives aimed at improving the health care, support, and services for veterans.

Document 1: Establishes in the Department of Veterans Affairs an Office of National Veterans Sports Programs and Special Events
to promote programs for the participation of disabled veterans in approved sporting and other events.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to reimburse any full-time board-certified health professional
for continuing professional education expenses incurred, up to $1,600 per year.
Document 3: Defines 'combat with the enemy' for purposes of proof of service-connection for veterans' disability compensation
as service on active duty in a theater of combat operations during a period of war.
Document 4: Requires the U.S. Postal Service to provide for the issuance and sale of a veterans health care stamp
to help fund the medical care and treatment of veterans.
Document 5: Reduces from three years to one year the period during which a disabled veterans' outreach program specialist
must complete the training program provided by the National Veterans' Employment and Training Services Institute.
Document 6: Amends the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act of 2014
to require the Department of Veterans Affairs to reimburse health care services provided to a veteran.

topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation and regulations concerning the exploration, production, and environmental impacts of oil and gas activities in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to repeal prohibitions against aggregating emissions from oil or gas exploration or production wells.
Requires the Administrator to issue a final rule adding hydrogen sulfide to the list of hazardous air pollutants.
Document 2: Prohibits any federal agency or official from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock.
Aims to reduce the effects of greenhouse gas emissions on climate change.
Document 3: Allows any area of the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) to be leased under the 2007-2012 oil and gas leasing program.
Effective upon the termination of such prohibition.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations for random testing of BOEMRE employees.
Prohibits the Secretary from exempting any category of activity of a vessel or facility engaged in OCS oil or gas drilling.
Document 5: Revokes all withdrawals of federal submerged lands from leasing for oil and natural gas exploration.

Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from granting an oil or natural gas lease for any OCS located within 25 miles of a state coastline unless the state has enacted a law approving the issuance of such leases.
Document 6: Authorizes the exploration, leasing, development, production of oil and gas in and from the Coastal Plain of Alaska.
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish a competitive oil and gas leasing program.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify provisions concerning tax credits for oil and gas well depletion.
Establishes the American-Made Energy Trust Fund.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Reform
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at reforming various aspects of U.S. immigration policy and practices.

Document 1: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996
Restores provisions of law amended by such provision
Document 2: Confers 'blue card status' upon an alien
Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
Withholds specified Department of Justice funds
Document 4: Establish a nonimmigrant visa category for an alien
Coming temporarily to the United States to work in the seafood processing industry
Document 5: Creates an exemption from the presumption that an alien seeking U.S. entry is an immigrant
Requires attestation from a health care provider
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a process for receiving H-2A nonimmigrant visas
Includes dairy workers in the H-2A category
Document 7: Directs the President to reduce foreign assistance allocations
Based on the number of unaccompanied alien children placed in federal custody

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons
topic.description: Legislation focused on the assessment and reporting of foreign countries' electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: EMP Weapons Accountability Assessment Act
report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons
Document 2: EMP Weapons Accountability Assessment Act
identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the designation, management, and conservation of wilderness areas and public lands in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey... parcels of National Forest System land that contain the cemeteries...
to ensure that such conveyances include unmarked gravesites and allow for the expansion of those cemeteries.
Document 2: Designates certain lands in the Jefferson National Forest as wilderness.
Sets forth requirements regarding ecological restoration.
Document 3: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park as wilderness.
Provides for the inclusion of specified potential wilderness land in the Wilderness.
Document 4: to support public purposes, including a limited agricultural exemption to allow for water quality and wildlife habitat improvements.
Document 5: to convey specified lands formerly part of the Fort Bayard Military Reservation... for business and community development.
Document 6: Establishes the Browns Canyon National Monument... to preserve the nationally significant biological, cultural, recreational... values found within the National Monument.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to complete a comprehensive management plan for the conservation and protection of the National Monument and the Wilderness.
Document 7: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management in Humboldt County, Nevada, as wilderness.
Sets forth provisions for the management of such land, including regarding livestock grazing, water rights, Native American tribal rights...

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving child welfare systems and protecting at-risk children through various reforms and funding initiatives.

Document 1: Promoting Accountability and Excellence in Child Welfare Act of 2011
comprehensive reforms to existing child welfare programs
Document 2: Supporting At-Risk Children Act
Strengthening and Finding Families for Children Act
Document 3: FVPSA Reauthorization Act of 2009
grant programs for specialized services for abused parents and their children
Document 4: Stop Child Abuse in Residential Programs for Teens Act of 2014
minimum standards if individually or together with other locations it has an effect on interstate commerce
Document 5: Violence Against Women Reauthorization Act of 2012
culturally specific services to mean community-based services
Document 6: Rehab and Ahmed Amer Foster Care Improvement Act of 2015
revise requirements that states must follow to contact the adult relatives of a child removed from custody
Document 7: Strengthening Outcomes for America's Juvenile and Family Courts Act of 2010
improve training, education, technical assistance, and research to assist courts, judges, judicial personnel

topic.name: Mental Health Services Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving mental health care access and services for various populations, including children, veterans, and individuals with co-occurring conditions.

Document 1: establish programs related to mental health services for children and adolescents
awarding scholarships to students who agree to work as child and adolescent mental health service professionals
Document 2: make permanent the special diabetes programs for Indians, which provide services for prevention and treatment of diabetes through the Indian Health Service and Indian health programs
Document 3: provide former members of the Armed Forces with an initial mental health assessment
health care services required to treat the former member's urgent mental health care needs
Document 4: authorize the Attorney General to make grants to states and other entities to develop, implement, or enhance veteran's treatment courts
programs that involve continuing judicial supervision over nonviolent offenders with substance abuse or mental health problems who have served in the U.S. military
Document 5: includes as a health care provider behavioral and mental health professionals
makes inpatient psychiatric hospitals eligible for payment for inpatient hospital services
Document 6: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants for services to children, adults, and older adults with mental illnesses
programs to address behavioral and mental health workforce needs in professional shortage areas
Document 7: authorize the use of specified funds to provide housing counseling and financial counseling for individuals before their release from institutions for individuals with mental illness
include in a state plan for comprehensive community-based mental health systems housing counseling and support services

topic.name: Prescription Drug Regulation and Access
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at improving the regulation, accessibility, and safety of prescription drugs and medical devices.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to promulgate regulations regarding the authorship, content, format, and dissemination requirements for patient medication information (PMI) for prescription drugs.
Requires such regulations to require the PMI for such a drug to be scientifically accurate and to include plain language that is not promotional in tone or content.
Document 2: This bill provides for Department of Veterans Affairs participation in state prescription drug monitoring programs.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to declare that nothing in it shall prevent or restrict the importation, distribution, or sale of investigational drugs or devices for terminally ill patients.
Prohibits the FDA Commissioner from requiring the disclosure, collection, or reporting of certain information concerning such drugs or devices.
Document 4: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to extend to manufacturers of generic drugs the requirement that drug manufacturers in certain circumstances pay an additional rebate to state Medicaid programs.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to replace the requirement that the FDA prioritize review of breakthrough medical devices with a requirement that the FDA establish a program to provide priority review for breakthrough medical devices.
The FDA must provide a summary of the basis for its determination regarding designation.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to require that all fees collected for the review of applications for human drugs, devices, animal drugs, or generic animal drugs be deposited in the general fund of the Treasury.
Requires the Secretary to establish the Center for Postmarket Drug, Device, and Biologic Safety and Effectiveness within the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
Document 7: This bill amends the Public Health Service Act and revises the Harold Rogers Prescription Drug Monitoring Program to require a state receiving grant funds for a prescription drug monitoring program to make relevant prescription information available to all individuals authorized by the state to prescribe controlled substances.

topic.name: Water Resource Management and Restoration
topic.description: Legislative initiatives focused on the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities and ecosystem restoration projects in various regions of the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior...
to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility and recycled water system.
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior...
to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities and to enhance and restore riparian habitat.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, in coordination with Los Angeles County, California, to prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration...
and other aspects of the San Gabriel River Watershed revitalization.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality...
in accordance with the recommendations of the report of the Chief of Engineers.
Document 7: Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement...
Sets forth provisions regarding the waiver, release, and retention of specified water rights claims.

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating mortgage practices and protecting homeowners in various financial situations.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require the servicer of a federally related mortgage to notify each holder of a subordinate lien on the property of a short sale request.
Requires a subordinate lien holder to respond in writing to the servicer within 45 days.
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information about a consumer credit transaction.
Excludes from the computation of such points and fees any escrow for future payment of insurance.
Document 3: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor, excluding certain debts from computation.
Allows modification of the rights of claim holders in the event of a foreclosure notice.
Document 4: Amends the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992 to modify requirements for annual reports on mortgage purchases and secondary market activities.
Requires the enterprises to serve underserved markets.
Document 5: Amends the National Housing Act to authorize the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to increase the maximum annual premium payments for mortgage insurance.
Establishes a Deputy Assistant Secretary for Risk Management and Regulatory Affairs.
Document 6: Establishes a standard for loan modifications or workout plans for pools of certain residential mortgage loans.
Declares that a servicer shall be deemed to be acting on behalf of the securitization vehicle in the best interest of all investors.
Document 7: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require a lender to make specified prior disclosures to a first-time borrower in connection with a consumer credit transaction.
Requires the consumer debtor to provide documentation that the consumer received home ownership counseling.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the conservation, research, and sustainable management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force
promote coral reef research and conservation
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of NOAA to the Secretary of Commerce
Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy
Document 3: expands the coral reef conservation program by awarding remaining program grant funds
directs NOAA to submit to Congress a national coral reef action strategy
Document 4: direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems
authorize the Secretary to conduct activities, including with local, regional, or international programs
Document 5: extends the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects
authorize actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function
Document 6: direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy
authorize the Secretary to make agreements with nonprofit organizations for public-private partnerships
Document 7: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds
establishes the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to coordinate federal actions that are designed to preserve and protect coral reef ecosystems

topic.name: Rural Development and Agriculture Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing rural education, energy, environmental quality, and agricultural support programs.

Document 1: grants to local educational agencies to improve rural after-school programs
priority to applicants serving students eligible for free or reduced price meals
Document 2: extend program funding through FY2018
provide loan guarantees and grants to agricultural producers for renewable energy system purchases
Document 3: provide technical assistance to Chesapeake Bay states for water quality goals
financial assistance to promote water quality goals in the Chesapeake Bay watershed
Document 4: extends and revises various agricultural programs including conservation and nutrition
community forest and open space conservation
Document 5: provide coverages for eligible crops based on individual yields
reduced premiums for limited resource and beginning producers
Document 6: allow schools to receive special assistance payments based on estimates of children eligible for free meals
requires schools to provide free meals to all students under certain conditions
Document 7: inclusion of locally produced agricultural food products in various programs
establish a wireless retailer electronic benefit transfer pilot program

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on promoting renewable energy sources, technologies, and related economic incentives.

Document 1: Amends the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978 to require a retail electric supplier to submit... specified federal renewable energy credits
Prescribes, for 2014 through 2039, a schedule of graduated annual percentages of a retail electric supplier's base amount that shall be generated from renewable energy resources.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Energy to facilitate competitions to develop... a direct drop-in replacement fuel for aviation

Establishes the Energy View Into Securing Independence for Our Nation (VISION) Commission; and the Energy VISION Fund to be used for the research and development of strategic unconventional fuels, solar and wind technology, renewable energy.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to prohibit the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from promulgating any regulation or guidance that limits or prohibits new carbon dioxide emissions from a fossil fuel-fired power plant

Requires the Administrator to review available control technology, safety considerations, and land and other resources available for production of fuels and other sources of energy used to propel vehicles.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of Commerce to establish a Clean Energy Technology Manufacturing and Export Assistance Program

Defines 'clean energy technology' to mean a technology related to the production, use, transmission, storage, control, or conservation of energy that is designed to... contribute to a stabilization of atmospheric greenhouse gas concentrations.
Document 5: Establishes within the Department of Energy (DOE) a national electric drive vehicle deployment program
Directs the Secretary to establish: an Advanced Batteries for Tomorrow program.
Document 6: Amends the Clean Air Act to require... the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to promulgate low carbon fuel performance standards for fuels and other sources of energy
Sets forth a declining standard that represents a graduated percentage reduction in aggregate emissions of GHG per Btu for each five-year period beginning in 2015.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2022 the tax credit for investment in solar energy property
Directs the Secretary of Energy to establish a voluntary voucher program, through 2015, for the purchase of plug-in electric vehicles.

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at protecting personally identifiable information and ensuring data security across different sectors.

Document 1: Requires entities to establish a privacy policy for personally identifiable information.
Authorizes the FTC to facilitate the development of harmonized privacy policy statements.
Document 2: Requires notification within two business days after a security breach involving personally identifiable information.
Document 3: Amends provisions to allow disclosure of personally identifiable information with informed consent.
Document 4: Requires notification of individuals whose sensitive personally identifiable information may have been accessed.
Document 5: Requires commercial entities to take reasonable measures to protect personal information and notify individuals of breaches.
Document 6: Requires educational institutions to establish information security practices to protect student records.
Document 7: Imposes penalties for concealing security breaches involving sensitive personally identifiable information.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating financial practices, enhancing accountability, and preventing fraud within the financial system.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to regulate tax return preparers
impose a $1,000 penalty for non-compliance
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of the Treasury to rely upon examinations conducted by state supervisory agencies
Empowers the Director of FinCen to furnish research for detecting financial crimes
Document 3: Prohibits performance awards for IRS employees with federal tax debt
Document 4: Directs uniform standards for the protection of Social Security numbers from fraud
Document 5: Revises requirements for anti-money laundering programs
Sets forth civil and criminal penalties for violations
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of State to publish a list of individuals responsible for financial crimes
Authorizes measures against individuals on such list
Document 7: Requires FTC to provide information on fraud targeting seniors
Establishes a competitive grant program for fraud prevention education

topic.name: Banking Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating banking practices, enhancing financial stability, and overseeing financial institutions in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 with respect to the prohibition against a banking entity's engaging in proprietary trading
exempts from the Volcker Rule any banking entity with total consolidated assets of $10 billion or less.
Document 2: amends the Financial Stability Act of 2010 to subject the Financial Stability Oversight Council to the Administrative Procedure Act
requires the Council to consider the appropriateness of imposing prudential standards to mitigate identified risks to U.S. financial stability.
Document 3: amends the Bank Service Company Act to allow the appropriate federal banking agency to authorize a state banking agency to make an examination of the bank service company.
Document 4: amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions
prescribes minimum leverage ratios for tier 1 capital maintained by a bank holding company.
Document 5: amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to repeal requirements for least-cost resolution of troubled insured depository institutions
repeals certain FDIC procedures for extension of time and mailed notification regarding determination of claims.
Document 6: directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to publish proposed revisions to the Small Bank Holding Company Policy Statement
exempts from certain escrow or impound requirements a loan secured by a first lien on a consumer's principal dwelling.

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation and Management
topic.description: Legislation and strategies aimed at the conservation of wildlife and their habitats, particularly in the context of climate change and hunting regulations.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to promulgate a national strategy for mitigating the impacts of global warming on wildlife populations
Establishes the National Global Warming and Wildlife Science Center to conduct scientific research on national issues related to the impacts of global warming on wildlife
Document 2: Prohibits the Department of the Interior from using any funds to make a determination on the listing of the northern long-eared bat under the Endangered Species Act
Document 3: Establishes the Wildlife and Hunting Heritage Conservation Council Advisory Committee to advise on wildlife and habitat conservation, hunting, and recreational shooting
Document 4: Requires an agency to ensure that hunting activities are allowed as a use of federal land
Specifies requirements for management plans regarding hunting activities
Document 5: Amends the Endangered Species Act by requiring state consent for adding species to the list of threatened or endangered plants and animals
Document 6: Requires the consent of the governor for a determination of endangered or threatened species status
Eliminates a rulemaking procedure for petitioning for species listing
Document 7: Requires cost estimates for compliance with the Endangered Species Act to be included in monthly billing statements

topic.name: Legislative Measures and Designations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures, including facility designations, amendments to existing acts, and conservation efforts.

Document 2: Amends the Transportation Equity Act for the 21st Century to modify the description of a high priority project.

Document 5: This bill amends the Highlands Conservation Act to reauthorize land conservation partnership projects.
Document 6: This bill requires the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to reinstate the construction license granted to Clark Canyon Hydro Company.

topic.name: Rail Transportation Safety and Development
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving safety standards and promoting development in rail transportation systems across the United States.

Document 1: establish an initiative to promote intercity and urban passenger rail operations
create rail projects qualified for federal incentives

perform at least two additional internal rail inspections per calendar year
Document 3: review the safety of all highway-rail grade crossings in the United States
establish a national database of information on the safety of highway-rail grade crossings
Document 5: reauthorizes appropriations to carry out rail safety activities for the safe transportation of hazardous materials
requires the Secretary to promulgate regulations to implement the requirement for a fatigue management plan
Document 7: prohibits the operation of a freight train or light locomotive engine used in the movement of freight unless it has a crew of at least two individuals

topic.name: STEM and Technology Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at promoting advancements in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) fields through research initiatives, workforce development, and immigration policy.

Document 7: Requires the National Science Foundation to award competitive grants to increase participation of women and minorities in STEM.
Activities include online workshops, mentoring programs, and outreach for underrepresented groups in STEM.

topic.name: Retirement and Savings Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing savings, retirement plans, and financial security for individuals and families.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to Coverdell education savings accounts
increase the maximum contribution limit in any taxable year from $2,000 to $10,000.
Document 2: amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA)
authorize a document of any type that is required or permitted to be furnished to a retirement plan participant
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax exemption for disability savings accounts
Allows tax-free distributions from such accounts for certain services
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to loans made from a qualified employer plan
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to modify regulations governing hardship distributions
Document 5: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) to require assets of single-employer pension plans to be held in trust
Sets forth additional requirements for plan termination
Document 6: authorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to pay burial and funeral expenses
accrued benefits, dependency and indemnity compensation
Document 7: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) to preclude federal preemption of a cause of action
Allows such a cause of action under state law against any employer or other plan sponsor

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support and funding for small businesses through various programs and amendments to the Small Business Act.

Document 1: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to reduce in 2008 the rate of certain loan fees imposed under the Small Business Act
Authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for direct loans and assistance under the SBA Microloan Program.
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act to authorize the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish a mentor-protege program for all small businesses

Prohibits a federal department or agency from carrying out a mentor-protege program for small businesses unless the head of such department or agency submits a program plan to the Administrator and the Administrator approves it.
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act to revise the Small Business Administration's criteria for awarding a grant to a women's business center
Includes the ability of the applicant to provide training and services to a representative number of unmarried women who are both socially and economically disadvantaged.
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act relating to the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Program

Requires certain program directives issued for the general conduct of SBIR programs within the federal government to provide that an agency may increase to specified levels amounts awarded in the first and second phases of the SBIR Program.
Document 5: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to reduce in 2008 the rate of certain loan fees imposed under the Small Business Act
Appropriates additional funds in FY2008 for loan subsidies under the SBA Business Loans Program Account.
Document 6: Establishes a three-year small business intermediary lending pilot program under which the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) may make direct loans to eligible nonprofit lending intermediaries
Authorizes the Administrator to make one percent, 20-year loans of up to $3 million to up to 20 lending intermediaries.
Document 7: Amends the Small Business Act to extend through FY2010 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
Increases, for both the SBIR and STTR programs, the individual small business award levels from $100,000 to $300,000.

topic.name: Tribal Governance and Recognition
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative measures related to the recognition, governance, and rights of Native American tribes in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes and ratifies the Pyramid Lake Paiute Tribe-Fish Springs Ranch 2013 Supplement to the 2007 Settlement Agreement
waiver and release against Fish Springs of all legal rights to challenge the validity, characteristics, or exercise of specified Fish Springs water rights
Document 2: authorize a tribal organization to establish a boxing commission
require boxing registration with the appropriate boxing commission of an Indian tribe
Document 3: Amends the Indian Self-determination and Education Assistance Act to revise tribal self-governance requirements
authorizes the Secretary of the Department of the Interior to select up to 50 new Indian tribes per year from those eligible to participate in self-governance
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to reimburse the Minnesota Chippewa Tribe for the amount, plus interest, that the Tribe contributed for the payment of attorneys' fees
requires the Tribe to provide the Secretary with updated membership rolls
Document 5: Extends federal recognition and associated services and benefits to the Mowa Band of Choctaw Indians of Alabama
restores federal rights and privileges abrogated by earlier statutes
Document 6: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana
makes the Tribe and each member eligible for all services and benefits provided by the United States to Indians
Document 7: directs the Department of Energy (DOE) to establish a financial assistance program to carry out eligible projects related to the modernization of the electric grid
